specific_location: Pine Ridge Natural Area in the woods adjacent to Dixon Reservoir.
description: This was a large myiarchus flycatcher with dark olive brown on the back and crown. The face and sides of the neck were similar in color to the crown but looked a bit more olive-gray. The bird had a dark gray throat and upper breast. Below the gray chest the sides and belly were fairly bright yellow as were the undertail coverts. There was an obvious contrast between the dark gray chest and the yellow belly. Heavy dark upper mandible, lower mandible also dark but with a pale base. Wings were darkish brown with two pale wing bars and pale edged inner tertials. The lower part of the primaries were rufous. Brownish-olive tail with rufous inner webs of the outer tail feathers. This was noticeable to me when the bird was facing me. When I was close to the bird it did raise its crown-feathers to form an obvious crest.Behaviors: This bird was very skittish and did not stay in one location for any length  of time. The first time I saw it, it flew between two cottonwoods. I then  found it sitting on an exposed lower branch of a cottonwood in a dense  thicket. It did "flycatch" one time at this location but then disappeared.  It took me over an hour to find it again and then it re-appeared on the same  exposed branch where I had seen it previously. Again, it sat for enough time  that I had excellent close views. It then flew away and a short time later,  appeared again at the same location. Although each of the three observations  was brief, the total time I saw it was probably about 15 minutes. After it  disappeared the third time and although I believe it stayed in the same  thicket, I was never able to find it again.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
